title: packera clevelandii var. heterophylla
intro: Packera clevelandii var. heterophylla, also known as Cleveland's ragwort or mountain ragwort, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Packera clevelandii var. heterophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
